# UC-19.3.2 · Storage Spaces Direct Pool Utilization and Tier Imbalance

## Description

S2D pools that skew toward capacity tier without enough flash cache increase latency for tiered volumes. Tracking pool free space and cache/capacity ratio preserves predictable VM performance under burst workloads.

## Value

S2D pools that skew toward capacity tier without enough flash cache increase latency for tiered volumes. Tracking pool free space and cache/capacity ratio preserves predictable VM performance under burst workloads.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest `Get-StoragePool` and tier capacity metrics on 15-minute cadence; (2) warn at 80% pool used and critical at 90%; (3) alert when cache share drops below policy for all-flash vs hybrid designs.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services, Azure Monitor metrics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=azure_stack_hci` `sourcetype="azurestackhci:s2d_pool"` with fields `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `used_pct`, `cache_tb`, `capacity_tb`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest `Get-StoragePool` and tier capacity metrics on 15-minute cadence; (2) warn at 80% pool used and critical at 90%; (3) alert when cache share drops below policy for all-flash vs hybrid designs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=azure_stack_hci sourcetype="azurestackhci:s2d_pool" earliest=-2h
| stats latest(used_pct) as used, latest(cache_tb) as cache_tb, latest(capacity_tb) as cap_tb by cluster_name, pool_name
| eval cache_share=round(100*cache_tb/nullif(cache_tb+cap_tb,0),1)
| where used>80 OR cache_share<15
| table cluster_name, pool_name, used, cache_share, cache_tb, cap_tb
| sort -used
```

Understanding this SPL

**Storage Spaces Direct Pool Utilization and Tier Imbalance** — S2D pools that skew toward capacity tier without enough flash cache increase latency for tiered volumes. Tracking pool free space and cache/capacity ratio preserves predictable VM performance under burst workloads.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=azure_stack_hci` `sourcetype="azurestackhci:s2d_pool"` with fields `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `used_pct`, `cache_tb`, `capacity_tb`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services, Azure Monitor metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: azure_stack_hci; **sourcetype**: azurestackhci:s2d_pool. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=azure_stack_hci, sourcetype="azurestackhci:s2d_pool", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_name, pool_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cache_share** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where used>80 OR cache_share<15` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Storage Spaces Direct Pool Utilization and Tier Imbalance**): table cluster_name, pool_name, used, cache_share, cache_tb, cap_tb
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the same scope in the vendor HCI or compute console (UCS, vCenter, Nutanix Prism, and so on).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (pool used %), Bar chart (cache vs capacity TB), Table (imbalanced pools).

## SPL

```spl
index=azure_stack_hci sourcetype="azurestackhci:s2d_pool" earliest=-2h
| stats latest(used_pct) as used, latest(cache_tb) as cache_tb, latest(capacity_tb) as cap_tb by cluster_name, pool_name
| eval cache_share=round(100*cache_tb/nullif(cache_tb+cap_tb,0),1)
| where used>80 OR cache_share<15
| table cluster_name, pool_name, used, cache_share, cache_tb, cap_tb
| sort -used
```

## Visualization

Gauge (pool used %), Bar chart (cache vs capacity TB), Table (imbalanced pools).
